target.name: Monarch Tractor
target.founder: Praveen Penmetsa, Mark Schwager, Carlo Mondavi, Zachary Omohundro

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $240M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Monarch Tractor is a Series C autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $240M+ to date. The relevant peer universe consists of six private companies in the same sector—including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Percepto—with no direct public comparables available.

Valuation triangulation was performed using the Stage-Progression method, the only applicable approach given data constraints. This method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $240M total capital raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $408M. A Peer-Median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow sector; the existing peer set lacked sufficient datapoints to support reliable median benchmarking without introducing unacceptable bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $347M to $469M, with a median point estimate of $408M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. The primary limitation is reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than market multiples or direct peer anchoring. Refinement would require either additional comparable transactions at Series C within autonomous systems, or public market precedent. Key variables affecting final valuation include competitive positioning within the autonomous agriculture subsegment and the company's path to revenue inflection.
